Quality-of-life release covering everything since 1.0.0: a self-maintaining
metadata & thumbnail engine, a self-describing update experience, automated
upkeep, and a mobile-friendly dashboard.

## Highlights

### Metadata & thumbnails, on autopilot
- **Reconciliation engine** — tracks desired vs. applied metadata per episode;
  when the One Pace dataset or episode-guide sheets change, only the episodes
  whose text actually changed are re-pushed to Plex. Runs automatically after
  every source refresh and ingest (`AUTO_RECONCILE`).
- **Thumbnail quality** — blank stills (fade frames, transparent PNGs, dangling
  404 references) are detected by pixel analysis and regenerated; when Plex
  keeps grabbing a bad frame, ffmpeg samples the episode and uploads the best
  frame itself.
- **Automatic poster updates** — the fan-made poster repo is re-checked daily
  (ETag-conditional) instead of only during a manual Full Plex sync.
- **Unified Library card** — coverage, metadata state, and thumbnail state in
  one per-arc view with Scan and Reconcile controls.

### Self-describing updates
- **What's New modal** — after an update, the dashboard shows the changelog
  entries added since your last visit; the navbar version button opens the full
  changelog anytime.
- **Update notifier** — the version button glows when a newer image has been
  published.

### Upkeep & polish
- **Nightly database backups** to `/data/backups/` (04:00 + boot catch-up,
  keeps 7).
- **Log filtering** — text + level filters over the live tail.
- **Mobile-friendly dashboard** — responsive pipeline table with compact
  download progress, stacking modals, and home-screen installability (web app
  manifest).
- Detailed tooltips on every control, DaisyUI theme picker fixes, and a long
  tail of correctness fixes (Plex refresh method, attempt pacing, tooltip
  clipping — see CHANGELOG.md for the full list).

## Docker

```
docker pull ghcr.io/dyonng/one-pace-plex-automator:1.1.0
```
